- From Statement 2, if George has access to a program (R), then he can compose (S). This can be written as R → S.
- From Statement 3, if George can compose (S) and wants to compose (Q), then he will compose (T). This can be written as S ∧ Q → T.

- Given S ∧ Q → T, if both S and Q are true, then T must be true.

- If P is true (George likes music), then Q is true (George wants to compose).
- If R is true (George has access to a program), then S is true (George can compose).
- Since Q is true and S is true, it follows that T is true (George will compose).
